query: Attention Is All You Need Vaswani arXiv first posted year
answer: "Attention Is All You Need" was first posted on arXiv in 2017. The paper introduced the Transformer model. It proposed a new network architecture based solely on attention mechanisms.

results.title: Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., ArXiv 2017)
results.snippet: To get context-dependence without recurrence we can use a network that applies attention multiple times over both input and output (as it is generated). The idea in this work is to use attention, applied multiple times, to get a network that is fast while still capturing positional information. * *Positional encoding*, which is a vector of the same length as the word representation, but that depends only on the position in the input. The more general form of this described in the paper has keys ($K$), queries ($Q$) and values ($V$), but the network uses the same vector for the key and value. Note, on the left hand side attention is over all input words, while on the right it is only over the words produced so far (ie. * Other details, (1) When the outputs are words, the vectors used to initially represent each input word are also used to represent the outputs and in the final linear transformation (though with some rescaling).

results.title: Attention Is All You Need - arXiv
results.snippet: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.

These convinced the team that the Transformer is a general-purpose language model, and not just good for translation.[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-:1-5). The team was named Team Transformer.[[9]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-wired-9). One key innovation was the use of an [attention mechanism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_(machine_learning) "Attention (machine learning)") which used neurons that multiply the outputs of other neurons, so-called _multiplicative units_.[[10]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-11) Neural networks using multiplicative units were later called _sigma-pi networks_[[11]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-PDP-12) or _[higher-order networks](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Higher-order\_neural\_network&action=edit&redlink=1 "Higher-order neural network (page does not exist)")_.[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Attention_Is_All_You_Need#cite_note-13) LSTM became the standard architecture for long sequence modelling until the 2017 publication of transformers.
